2604.17105.title: How Tokenization Limits Phonological Knowledge Representation in Language Models and How to Improve Them
2604.17105.paper_summary: Tokenization is the first step in every language model (LM), yet it never takes the sounds of words into account. We investigate how tokenization influences text-only LMs' ability to represent phonological knowledge. Through a series of probing experiments, we show that subword-based tokenization systematically weakens the encoding of both local (e.g., rhyme) and global (e.g., syllabification) phonological features. To quantify this effect, we introduce the syllabification-tokenization alignment distance (STAD), a metric that measures the misalignment between a model's tokenization and the natural syllable boundaries of words, and find that higher misalignment correlates with poorer phonological representations, providing a simple diagnostic for phonology-aware tokenization. To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight IPA-based fine-tuning method that infuses phonological awareness into LMs, leading to consistent improvements across three phonology-related tasks while largely preserving math and general reasoning ability, with 1.1\% and 0.9\% drops on GSM8K and MMLU, respectively.
2604.17105.paper_summary_zh: 標記化是每個語言模型（LM）中的第一步，但它從不考慮單詞的聲音。我們調查標記化如何影響僅文本的 LM 表示音韻知識的能力。通過一系列探測實驗，我們顯示基於子詞的標記化系統性地削弱了對局部（例如，押韻）和全局（例如，音節劃分）音韻特徵的編碼。為了量化這一效果，我們引入了音節劃分-標記化對齊距離（STAD），這是一個測量模型標記化與單詞自然音節邊界之間不對齊的指標，並發現更高的不對齊與較差的音韻表示相關，提供了一個簡單的音韻感知標記化診斷工具。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了一種輕量級的基於 IPA 的微調方法，將音韻意識注入 LM，從而在三個與音韻相關的任務中實現了一致的改進，同時在數學和一般推理能力方面基本保持不變，GSM8K 和 MMLU 的下降分別為 1.1% 和 0.9%。
2604.17105.author: Disen Liao et.al.
